title: podman-remote: run --pod foo: error preparing container
body:
Window 1:
```console
$ ./bin/podman system service --timeout=0 unix:/tmp/mypodmansock
```
Window 2:
```console
$ ./bin/podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/mypodmansock pod create --name test
c1506b1b2e04f6e1542405c157f5296fdf31fae1e257e42701c8c0a036dd924b
$ ./bin/podman-remote --url unix:/tmp/mypodmansock run --pod test --rm alpine date
Error: error preparing container c31c56dc406b4a648f78b0bd70149685084a3d03aeb3a94a25e7a6ea6f073ac0 for attach: some dependencies of container c31c56dc406b4a648f78b0bd70149685084a3d03aeb3a94a25e7a6ea6f073ac0 are not started: 0179e3fc8f9f882c9050ac0b5bb79f9ead6fb4e05c2bd5aff67e8830dfd022cb: container state improper
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND  CREATED         STATUS   PORTS  NAMES
c31c56dc406b  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  date     21 seconds ago  Created         upbeat_ishizaka
0179e3fc8f9f  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                      54 seconds ago  Created         c1506b1b2e04-infra
```

Seems to be both root and rootless.

comments.body: I thought we fixed this... It's almost certainly not doing a recursive start when it's part of a pod.
this is coming from attach code, so assigning to @mheon
